name: Chemical Coordination and Integration
full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Chemical Coordination and Integration

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Chemical Coordination and Integration

questions.question: Which of the following is not a mixed/composite gland?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is not a mixed/composite gland?
questions.explanation: A gland that contains both exocrine and endocrine portions is called a mixed or composite gland. These glands secrete both hormones and enzymes. The pineal gland is an example of an endocrine gland.

questions.question: By chemical nature, hormones are never:
questions.question_raw: By chemical nature, hormones are never:
questions.explanation: Hormones are generally protein components having amino acids as well as steroids. Hormones are generally not monosaccharide derivatives in nature.

questions.question: Glucocorticoids regulate the metabolism of:
questions.question_raw: Glucocorticoids regulate the metabolism of:
questions.explanation: The name glucocorticoid (glucose + cortex + steroid) is composed of its role in the regulation of glucose metabolism, synthesis in the adrenal cortex, and its steroidal structure. The principal glucocorticoid hormone is cortisol, which regulates the metabolism of proteins, carbohydrates, and lipids.

questions.question: The hormone secreted by pars intermedia of hypophysis is:
questions.question_raw: The hormone secreted by pars intermedia of hypophysis is:
questions.explanation: The hormone secreted by pars intermedia of hypophysis is melanocyte-stimulating hormones (MSH). MSH is a peptide hormone also known as melanotropins.

questions.question: Adrenal medulla consists of a cluster of ________ nerves and those cells are also known as ________ cells.
questions.question_raw: Adrenal medulla consists of a cluster of ________ nerves and those cells are also known as ________ cells.
questions.explanation: The adrenal medulla consists of a cluster of sympathetic nerves and those cells are also known as chromaffin cells. The adrenal medulla is part of the adrenal gland located at the center of the adrenal gland.

questions.question: Process of parturition is facilitated by:
questions.question_raw: Process of parturition is facilitated by:
questions.explanation: The process of parturition begins after the completion of the gestation period. The muscles present in the uterus start contracting due to the release of hormone Relaxin and Oxytocin.

questions.question: The largest endocrine gland (only) is:
questions.question_raw: The largest endocrine gland (only) is:
questions.explanation: The thyroid is the largest endocrine gland present in the human body. This gland release thyroxin hormone that helps in the metabolism of carbohydrates, fats, and proteins.

questions.question:
Match the following :
 Hormone
 Nature
 a) Parathormone
 i) Polypeptide
 b) Oxytocin
 ii) Amino acid Derivative
 c) Sex hormone
 iii) Peptide
 d) Norepinephrine
 iv) Steroid
questions.question_raw:
Match the following :
 

Hormone Nature
a) Parathormone i) Polypeptide
b) Oxytocin ii) Amino acid Derivative
c) Sex hormone iii) Peptide
d) Norepinephrine iv) Steroid
questions.explanation: Parathormoneis a polypeptide hormone. Oxytocin is peptide hormone. Sex hormone is a steroid hormone and Norepinephrine is a amino acid derivative.

questions.question: Old people usually exhibit weaker immune power due to degeneration of:
questions.question_raw: Old people usually exhibit weaker immune power due to degeneration of:
questions.explanation: The thymus gland produces a hormone called thymosin which stimulates the T-cells. T-cells help in producing antibodies in the bodies. In older people, weaker immune power is due to the degeneration of the thymus gland.

questions.question: Thyroid gland do not secrete______.
questions.question_raw: Thyroid gland do not secrete______.
questions.explanation: The melatonin hormone is secreted by Pineal Gland.

questions.question: The hormone causing release of milk after the birth of young one is:
questions.question_raw: The hormone causing release of milk after the birth of young one is:
questions.explanation: Mammary glands start releasing milk after parturition. The hormone causing the release of milk after the birth of the young one is Oxytocin.

questions.question: Thermostat for the body is:
questions.question_raw: Thermostat for the body is:
questions.explanation: The body keeps its core temperature constant at about 37^oC by physiological adjustment controlled by Hypothalamus, the so-called thermostat center.

questions.question: A hormone which acts on the hypothalamus to decrease food intake, stimulate satiety, and increase energy output is:
questions.question_raw: A hormone which acts on the hypothalamus to decrease food intake, stimulate satiety, and increase energy output is:
questions.explanation: Leptin hormone acts on the hypothalamus to decrease food intake, stimulate satiety, and increase energy output. Leptin hormone is made by adipose cells.

questions.question:
Match the following :
 Hormone
 Target tissue/cell
 a) Follicle stimulating hormone
 i) liver
 b) Luteinising hormone
 ii) kidney tubule
 c) Cortisol
 iii)sertoli cells
 d) Aldosterone
 iv) interstitial cells
questions.question_raw:
Match the following :

Hormone Target tissue/cell
a) Follicle stimulating hormone i) liver
b) Luteinising hormone ii) kidney tubule
c) Cortisol iii)sertoli cells
d) Aldosterone iv) interstitial cells
questions.explanation: Follicle stimulating hormone acts on sertoli cells. Luteinsing hormone acts on interstitial cells. Cortisol cells acts on liver and Aldosterone hormone acts on kidney tubule.

questions.question:
Read the following:
 The major pineal hormone is melatonin.
 In normal human beings, melatonin secretion increases in light and decreases in dark.
 Abnormally high levels of melatonin in children cause delayed sexual development.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following:

  1. The major pineal hormone is melatonin.
  2. In normal human beings, melatonin secretion increases in light and decreases in dark.
  3. Abnormally high levels of melatonin in children cause delayed sexual development.
questions.explanation: Melatonin is a hormone secreted by the pineal gland in the brain. It helps to regulate other hormones and maintains the body circadian rhythm. Abnormally high levels of melatonin in children cause delayed sexual development.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Chemical Coordination and Integration

questions.question: Insulin is ________ hormone.
questions.question_raw: Insulin is ________ hormone.
questions.explanation: Insulin is an anabolic hormone because this hormone helps in the conversion of glucose into glycogen.

questions.question: Which of the following hormones will stimulate gluconeogenesis?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following hormones will stimulate gluconeogenesis?
questions.explanation: Glucocorticoids stimulate gluconeogenesis. Similarly, glucagon also stimulates the process of gluconeogenesis which also contributes to hyperglycemia.

questions.question:
Shekhar is 3 years old and he lives in hilly areas. He has swollen neck:
 He is suffering from goitre caused due to iodine deficiency which may lead to cretinism.
 Goitre may lead him to myxoedema and Grave’s disease in the future.
questions.question_raw:
Shekhar is 3 years old and he lives in hilly areas. He has swollen neck:

  1. He is suffering from goitre caused due to iodine deficiency which may lead to cretinism.
  2. Goitre may lead him to myxoedema and Grave’s disease in the future.

options.label: B
options.text: Statement (ii) is correct and (i) is incorrect

options.label: D
options.text: Statement (ii) is incorrect and (i) is correct

correct_option_texts: Statement (ii) is incorrect and (i) is correct
questions.explanation: The persons living in hilly areas generally suffer from goiter disease. Goiter is caused due to iodine deficiency which may lead to cretinism. Goiter never leads to myxedema or hypothyroidism.

questions.question: A chemical acts slowly, requiring hours to take effect. It may pass in and out of many cells in which they have no effect. When they find appropriate receptors in their target cell they bind to it. Chemical is:
questions.question_raw: A chemical acts slowly, requiring hours to take effect. It may pass in and out of many cells in which they have no effect. When they find appropriate receptors in their target cell they bind to it. Chemical is:
questions.explanation: Aldosterone hormone is a chemical that acts slowly, requires hours to take effect. It may pass in and out of many cells in which they have no effect. When they find appropriate receptors in their target cell they bind to it to bring about changes.

questions.question:
Match the following:
 Hormone
 Synonym
 (a) Adrenaline
 (i) birth hormone
 (b) Oxytocin
 (ii) moulting hormone
 (c) Ecdysone
 (iii) luteotropic hormone
 (d) Prolactin
 (iv) fight or flight hormone
questions.question_raw:
Match the following:

Hormone Synonym
(a) Adrenaline (i) birth hormone
(b) Oxytocin (ii) moulting hormone
(c) Ecdysone (iii) luteotropic hormone
(d) Prolactin (iv) fight or flight hormone
questions.explanation: Adrenaline hormone is called fight and flight hormone. Oxytocin is called the birth hormone. Ecdysone is called moulting hormone and prolactin is called a luteotropic hormone.

questions.question: A dwarf person showing stunted growth and mental retardation both is a result of hyposecretion of:
questions.question_raw: A dwarf person showing stunted growth and mental retardation both is a result of hyposecretion of:
questions.explanation: Hypothyroidism causes defective development and maturation of the growing baby leading to stunted growth (cretinism), mental retardation, low intelligence quotient, abnormal skin, deaf-mutism, etc.

questions.question:
Match the names of organ having isolated endocrine cells with hormone secreted by those cells :
 Organ
 Hormone
 a) Kidney
 i) angiotensin
 b) Liver
 ii) Erythropoietin
 c) Gastro-intestinal Tract
 iii) ANF
 d) Heart
 iv) CCK
questions.question_raw:
Match the names of organ having isolated endocrine cells with hormone secreted by those cells :

Organ Hormone
a) Kidney i) angiotensin
b) Liver ii) Erythropoietin
c) Gastro-intestinal Tract iii) ANF
d) Heart iv) CCK
questions.explanation:
The atrial wall of our heart secretes a very important peptide hormone called ANF.
Angiotensin I is produced by the action of renin (an enzyme produced by the kidneys) on a protein called angiotensinogen, which is formed by the liver.
The juxtaglomerular cells of kidney produce a peptide hormone called erythropoietin.
The gastro-intestinal tract secrete peptide hormone called CCK.

questions.question:
Match the following:
 Column A
 Column B
 (a) PTH
 (i) Mineralocorticoid
 (b) Cortisol
 (ii) Inhibiting hormone
 (c) Aldosterone
 (iii) Collip’s hormone
 (d) Somatostatin
 (iv) Glucocorticoid
questions.question_raw:
Match the following:

Column A Column B
(a) PTH (i) Mineralocorticoid
(b) Cortisol (ii) Inhibiting hormone
(c) Aldosterone (iii) Collip’s hormone
(d) Somatostatin (iv) Glucocorticoid
questions.explanation: Pituitary thyroid hormone is also called as collip’s hormone. Cortisol is called a glucocorticoid hormone. Aldosterone is called as mineralocorticoid and somatostatin is called an inhibiting hormone.

questions.question: The Prolactin inhibiting neurohormone is:
questions.question_raw: The Prolactin inhibiting neurohormone is:
questions.explanation: The Prolactin inhibiting neurohormone is dopamine. Dopamine is a neurotransmitter that helps control the brain’s reward and pleasure centers.

questions.question: Pituitary dwarfs will not show:
questions.question_raw: Pituitary dwarfs will not show:
questions.explanation: The pituitary dwarf hormone will show normal intelligence, stunning growth, and inhibited the growth of long bones. Pituitary dwarfs will no show mental retardation.

questions.question:
Read the following statements about gestational diabetes:
 It must be controlled to protect the baby’s growth and development.
 It puts women at risk for developing Type - 1 diabetes.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following statements about gestational diabetes:

  1. It must be controlled to protect the baby’s growth and development.
  2. It puts women at risk for developing Type - 1 diabetes.

options.label: B
options.text: Statement (ii) is correct and (i) is incorrect

options.label: D
options.text: Statement (ii) is incorrect and (i) is correct

correct_option_texts: Statement (ii) is incorrect and (i) is correct
questions.explanation: Gestational diabetes develops during pregnancy. It must be controlled to protect the baby’s growth and development. This kind of diabetes never leads to develop type-1 diabetes.

questions.question: Oral contraceptive pills contain estrogen and progesterone, that prevent:
questions.question_raw: Oral contraceptive pills contain estrogen and progesterone, that prevent:

options.label: B
options.text: release of a secondary oocyte from the ovary.

options.label: D
options.text: implantation of a fertilized egg in the uterus.

correct_option_texts: release of a secondary oocyte from the ovary.
questions.explanation: The oral contraceptive pill contains estrogen and progesterone that prevents the release of a secondary oocyte from the ovary. The secondary oocyte develops into a female egg or ovum. The use of these pills prevents the ovum formation.

questions.question: To exert their effect on the target cell, water-soluble hormones will need to bind with ______.
questions.question_raw: To exert their effect on the target cell, water-soluble hormones will need to bind with ______.
questions.explanation: Hormones are released in trace amounts from endocrine glands and to exert their effect on the target cell, water-soluble hormones will need to bind with cell membrane-bound receptors.

questions.question: During the adolescence phase, Sunil’s body became abnormally large due to the excessive growth of bones of his lower jaw and limbs. This abnormality is called:
questions.question_raw: During the adolescence phase, Sunil’s body became abnormally large due to the excessive growth of bones of his lower jaw and limbs. This abnormality is called:
questions.explanation: Acromegaly is the abnormal growth of the hands, feet, and face caused by overproduction of growth hormone by the pituitary gland.

questions.question: Which of the following is not matched correctly?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is not matched correctly?

options.label: B
options.text: Thyroid and Parathyroid $ \to $ derived from ectoderm

questions.explanation: The adrenal cortex is mesodermal in origin. The adrenal medulla is derived from ectoderm, gonads are derived from mesoderm, thyroid, and parathyroids derived ectoderm.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Chemical Coordination and Integration

questions.question: The following slide is of Transverse section of the thyroid gland and it shows [from left to right] :
questions.question_raw: The following slide is of Transverse section of the thyroid gland and it shows [from left to right] :

options.label: A
options.text: C-cells, colloid containing T3&T4, parafollicular cells

options.label: B
options.text: follicular epithelium, colloid containing thyroglobulin, clear cells

options.label: C
options.text: parafollicular cells, colloid containing thyroxine&TCT, follicular epithelium

options.label: D
options.text: epithelium, colloid containing T4&TCT, parafollicular cells

correct_option_texts: follicular epithelium, colloid containing thyroglobulin, clear cells
questions.explanation: Transverse section of thyroid gland shows mark (1) as follicular epithelium, mark (2) as colloid containingthyroglobuin and mark (3) as clear cells.

questions.question:
Following conversion in forward and backward directions is regulated as :
$(i) \to \ \Leftrightarrow \ \leftarrow (ii)$
Blood calcium Bone calcium
questions.question_raw:
Following conversion in forward and backward directions is regulated as :
{tex}(i) \to \ \Leftrightarrow \ \leftarrow (ii){/tex}
Blood calcium Bone calcium
questions.explanation: Thyrocalcitonin (TCT) influence the metabolism of calcium inside the body. Parathyroid hormone (PTH) absorption of calcium in bone.

questions.question: To exert their effect on the target cell, water-soluble hormones will need to bind with:
questions.question_raw: To exert their effect on the target cell, water-soluble hormones will need to bind with:
questions.explanation: Hormone receptors present on the cell membrane of the target cells are called membrane-bound receptors and the receptors present inside the target cell are called intracellular receptors, mostly nuclear receptors (present in the nucleus). Binding of a hormone to its receptor leads to the formation of a hormone-receptor complex.

questions.question:
Secretion of aldosterone is stimulated by:
 the decrease in sodium ion concentration in the plasma.
 the decrease in potassium ion concentration in the plasma.
 the decrease in the volume of blood.
questions.question_raw:
Secretion of aldosterone is stimulated by:

  1. the decrease in sodium ion concentration in the plasma.
  2. the decrease in potassium ion concentration in the plasma.
  3. the decrease in the volume of blood.
questions.explanation: If decreased blood pressure is detected, the adrenal gland is stimulated by these stretch receptors to release aldosterone, which increases sodium reabsorption from the urine, sweat, and the gut. This causes increased osmolarity in the extracellular fluid, which will eventually return blood pressure toward normal.

questions.question:
Read the following statements about suprarenal glands:
 A suprarenal gland represents two endocrine glands arranged in one organ.
 It’s outer region secretes steroid hormones.
 Also known as a gland of emergency.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following statements about suprarenal glands:

  1. A suprarenal gland represents two endocrine glands arranged in one organ.
  2. It’s outer region secretes steroid hormones.
  3. Also known as a gland of emergency.
questions.explanation: The adrenal glands (also known as suprarenal glands) are endocrine glands that produce a variety of hormones including adrenaline and the steroids aldosterone and cortisol. They are found above the kidneys. Also Known as the emergency gland of the body. This is because it produces a hormone called ADRENALIN which prepares our body from emergencies.

questions.question: A person accidentally got his adrenal gland injured. He died and reports will probably reveal that death was caused due to:
questions.question_raw: A person accidentally got his adrenal gland injured. He died and reports will probably reveal that death was caused due to:

options.label: A
options.text: Loss of both adrenal medulla function and adrenal cortex function

questions.explanation: The adrenal cortex is situated along the perimeter of the adrenal gland. The adrenal cortex mediates the stress response through the production of aldosterone and cortisol. If a person got injured his adrenal gland may die due to loss of stress response which will be revealed in the report.

questions.question: Which of the following scientists won the Nobel prize for discovering second messengers?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following scientists won the Nobel prize for discovering second messengers?
questions.explanation:
Second messengers are molecules that relay signals received at receptors on the cell surface to trigger physiological changes.
The Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine 1971 was awarded to Earl W. Sutherland, Jr. "for his discoveries concerning the mechanisms of the action of hormones".

questions.question: Which of the following gas is found to work as a second messenger?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following gas is found to work as a second messenger?
questions.explanation: Second messengers are molecules that relay signals received at receptors on the cell surface to trigger physiological changes. Nitrogen oxide (NO) acts as a second messenger.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:
 the endocrine system corrects the imbalance originally detected by the nervous system.
 nerve cells and endocrine cells exist side by side in the adrenal gland and pituitary.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following statements:

  1. the endocrine system corrects the imbalance originally detected by the nervous system.
  2. nerve cells and endocrine cells exist side by side in the adrenal gland and pituitary.

options.label: C
options.text: Statement (i) is incorrect and (ii) is correct

options.label: D
options.text: Statement (i) is correct and (ii) is incorrect

questions.explanation: The endocrine system corrects the imbalance originally detected by the nervous system. Nerve cells and endocrine cells exist side by side in the adrenal gland and pituitary gland.

questions.question: Glucose will be lost in the urine of patients suffering from:
questions.question_raw: Glucose will be lost in the urine of patients suffering from:

options.label: D
options.text: Both diabetes mellitus and diabetes insipidus

questions.explanation: Glucose will be lost in the urine of patients suffering from diabetes mellitus. It is a chronic lifelong condition that affects our body ability to use the energy found in the blood.

questions.question: The correct matched pair out of the following is:
questions.question_raw: The correct matched pair out of the following is:
questions.explanation: Glucagon hormone released from the pancreas increase the sugar level in blood in case deficiency of glucose. Parathyroid hormone is not hypocalcemia and TSH is not a neurohormone.

questions.question:
Tanay is diagnosed with a tumor in his parathyroid. He will show out of the following symptoms:
 excretion of calcium in urine
 osteoporosis
 tetany
 deposition of calcium in kidney tubules
questions.question_raw:
Tanay is diagnosed with a tumor in his parathyroid. He will show out of the following symptoms:

  1. excretion of calcium in urine
  2. osteoporosis
  3. tetany
  4. deposition of calcium in kidney tubules
questions.explanation: Parathyroid hormone is released from parathyroid glands that regulate calcium ions in the body. The person suffering from a tumor of parathyroid shows symptoms of osteoporosis and deposition of calcium in kidney tubules.

questions.question: Some girls develop male secondary sexual characters like beard, moustaches, hoarse voice, etc. This is called ________.
questions.question_raw: Some girls develop male secondary sexual characters like beard, moustaches, hoarse voice, etc. This is called ________.
questions.explanation: Development of secondary sexual characters like beard, moustaches, hoarse voice, etc. in girls is called adrenal virilism. This occurs due to the excessive secretion of the adrenaline hormone.

questions.question:
Match the following :
 Disease 
 Hormone source responsible
 a) Cushion syndrome
 i) hyposecretions from pars nervosa
 b) Diabetes mellitus
 ii) hyposecretion of mineralocorticoids
 c) Diabetes insipidus
 iii) hyposecretion of glucocorticoids
 d) Addison’s disease
 iv) hyposecretion from beta cells of pancreas
questions.question_raw:
Match the following :

Disease Hormone source responsible
a) Cushion syndrome i) hyposecretions from pars nervosa
b) Diabetes mellitus ii) hyposecretion of mineralocorticoids
c) Diabetes insipidus iii) hyposecretion of glucocorticoids
d) Addison’s disease iv) hyposecretion from beta cells of pancreas
questions.explanation: Cushing’s syndrome occurs hypo-secretion of glucocorticoids hormone. Diabetes mellitus is due to hypo-secretion of beta cells of pancreas. Diabetes insipidus is due to hypo-secretion form pars nervosa and Addison’s disease is due to hypo-secretion of mineral corticoids.

questions.question: Girls blind from birth attain puberty earlier than normal due to:
questions.question_raw: Girls blind from birth attain puberty earlier than normal due to:

options.label: A
options.text: Non inhibition of growth hormone by melatonin

options.label: C
options.text: Ovaries of inborn blind girls is quite different from other girls

questions.explanation: The pineal gland releases Melatonin (a hormone) which is responsible for inhibiting the Luteinising hormone and Follicle Stimulating Hormone that brings about puberty. Melatonin is secreted more in absence of light. In normal humans, it is more released at night when it is released more. In blind girls, light is absorbed more at day whose effect continues at night. Hence, Melatonin is secreted less and LH and FSH are not inhibited more, and puberty is experienced early.
